name: fleet_health_report
description: Generate a fleet health report across all tenants
prompt: Check the flock — give me a full fleet health report
expected_behaviors: Reads references/di-api-reference.md for endpoint details
Calls GET /devices/ with pagination to enumerate all devices
Calls GET /multitenancy/tenant/ to get tenant names
Groups devices by tenant, connectivity_status, and deployment_status
Identifies stale devices (EXPIRED or no check-in > 7 days)
Identifies version drift (brain_version comparison across fleet)
Creates output directory ~/Documents/claude-code/di-shepherd/reports/
Reads icci-report-branding repo for report formatting
Generates ICCI-branded PDF report with stat grid, tenant breakdown tables, and findings
Saves report as ICCI-DI-FleetHealth_DDMMMYY.pdf

name: false_positive_remediation
description: Handle a false-positive detection by closing the event
prompt: We have a false positive on event 12345 — it's a legitimate updater. Close it.
expected_behaviors: Calls GET /events/12345 to retrieve full event details
Shows the event details: file hash, filename, severity, device, tenant
Asks for explicit confirmation before closing
Calls POST /events/actions/close with the event ID
Logs the action to ~/Documents/claude-code/di-shepherd/audit-log.jsonl
Verifies the event was closed by re-querying
Does NOT add to allow list (our key lacks FULL_ACCESS)

name: version_drift_watchover
description: Check for agent/brain version drift across the fleet
prompt: Are any of our endpoints running old brain versions?
expected_behaviors: Calls GET /devices/ with pagination to get all devices
Extracts brain_version from each device
Determines the fleet majority version
Flags devices more than 1 major version behind
Groups flagged devices by tenant for easy triage
Presents findings with device hostname, current version, expected version, tenant
Suggests remediation (update via DI console — no API endpoint for forced updates)
